summary: First iteration testing of Simple Inventory System (React + FastAPI + MongoDB). Backend: 13/14 pytest tests passed (92.8%). Frontend: All 12 critical E2E flows passed via Playwright (100%). One real backend bug found: brute force lockout is ineffective behind the Kubernetes ingress because the identifier uses request.client.host which is the ingress pod IP (rotates across pods), so failed attempts for a single user split across many identifiers and never reach the 5-fail threshold.

critical.issue: Brute force lockout (5-fail → 429) does NOT trigger in the production/preview environment. Cause: identifier is built as f'{request.client.host}:{email}', but behind the Kubernetes ingress request.client.host is the ingress pod IP and rotates across pods (observed 10.208.130.77 and 10.208.130.78 alternating within the same Session). The per-pod counters each stay below MAX_FAILED=5. Fix: derive client IP from X-Forwarded-For (first value) when present, or key the identifier on email only (with appropriate per-account rate limiting). Verified by 6 wrong-password POSTs that all returned 401 instead of one returning 429.

action_items: Backend: Fix brute-force lockout identifier to honor X-Forwarded-For (or rate-limit by email only) so login_attempts counter is not split per ingress pod IP. After fix, the existing test TestAuth.test_brute_force_lockout should pass.
critical_code_review_comments: auth: brute force identifier uses request.client.host which is unreliable behind a reverse proxy/ingress; must use X-Forwarded-For (request.headers.get('x-forwarded-for', '').split(',')[0].strip()) for real client IP
cookies: secure=True + samesite='none' is correct for cross-site preview URL; works as expected — cookies set on login/register and cleared on logout

products: stats route is defined AFTER the {product_id} dynamic routes within the same router; works currently only because stats is GET and dynamic routes are PATCH/DELETE (no method conflict), but if a GET /products/{id} is ever added, /products/stats would be shadowed. Consider reordering or naming.
products PATCH /{id}: empty update body returns 400 with 'No fields to update' which is fine; consider 422 for consistency with Pydantic validation responses
server.py: monolithic ~415 line file; acceptable for MVP size but split into routers/services if it grows
MongoDB _id is properly excluded everywhere (serialize_user / serialize_product). Good.
Frontend: api.js uses process.env.REACT_APP_BACKEND_URL with withCredentials:true correctly; cookie-based auth works across the preview URL.
Frontend: ProductDialog accepts string values in number inputs and parses on submit — validation works, no issues found
seed_data_creation: Tests create their own products prefixed with TEST_ and clean up in fixtures. A few TEST_ users were created by register/lockout tests and left behind (acceptable since email is unique-per-run UUID).
context_for_next_testing_agent: Backend tests live at /app/backend/tests/backend_test.py. Run with: pytest /app/backend/tests/backend_test.py -v --tb=short --junitxml=/app/test_reports/pytest/pytest_results.xml. Frontend works end-to-end (login, add, edit, increment/decrement with disable-at-0, low-stock flag at qty<5, search, delete confirm, theme toggle persists in localStorage, logout). Only outstanding issue is the brute force lockout requiring proxy-aware client-IP extraction.
rca of the issue: Brute force lockout RCA: backend uses identifier = f'{request.client.host}:{email}'. In Kubernetes preview env, traffic is proxied through ingress; request.client.host reports the ingress pod IP (10.208.130.77/78 alternating). Consequently 6 consecutive wrong-password POSTs to /api/auth/login from one client got distributed across multiple identifiers, leaving each below the MAX_FAILED=5 threshold and never triggering the 429 lockout branch. Reproduction: requests.Session().post(login, wrong pw) x 6 → all return 401. Mitigation: in get-real-client-ip helper, use X-Forwarded-For header first value if present (FastAPI behind proxy pattern), and fall back to request.client.host.
